findings.title: Unescaped DB string ({item.name}) rendered as JSX child in an innerHTML-applied fragment
findings.description: Identical pattern to steps 04/05. ProductList.render (L28) interpolates the free-form DB string `{item.name}` as a JSX child; the @jiso/server JSX runtime does not escape text children (packages/server/src/jsx-runtime.ts L79-84), only attributes. ProductList is fragmentTarget:true and is emitted both via the mutation-response fragment and the deferred stream chunk (app.ts L145, L176, L208 — renderShopPageDeferredStream / submitAddToCart), all applied client-side via innerHTML / insertAdjacentHTML with no sanitization (packages/runtime/src/response-fragment-apply.ts L58, L62). Markup in a product name becomes stored XSS. Not directly exploitable here because names are static fixtures and addToCart never writes a name from input — MEDIUM/medium confidence; rises to HIGH once names are admin/vendor/user-controlled. Reference examples/commerce avoids this (renders only id/stock as children; escapeHtml on free-form data). The scanner's insecure-crypto flag on L11/L42 is a false positive (comments; CSRF crypto is correct HMAC-SHA256).
findings.recommendation: Escape DB/user strings rendered as children, e.g. `{escapeHtml(item.name)}`, or fix the JSX runtime to auto-escape string text children while preserving a raw type for pre-rendered HTML.

refusal.reason: Analyzed all five target files fully, plus traced the relevant framework dataflow (jsx-runtime, html, csrf, response-fragment-apply) and compared against the reference commerce app. The two generated/ files were returned with empty findings per the explicit out-of-scope rule for generated artifacts, not due to discomfort.
